Article Title: Congress demands independent probe into donation chori
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Leaders of opposition Mallikarjun Kharge and Rahul Gandhi Sunday shot off a letter to PM Narendra Modi demanding an independent probe into the Ram temple donation issue and warning that his govt's credibility depends on its swift action - putting the controversy high on the opposition's agenda for Parliament's monsoon session that may lead to confrontation with govt.Also, with Cockroach Janta Party and activist Sonam Wangchuk planned to march to Parliament Monday over paper leaks and to demand the resignation of the education minister, many opposition parties have supported the protest, and are likely to turn up at Jantar Mantar for the Parliament gherao.The twin issues, on which the opposition is seeking a discussion, are likely to dominate the first week of the session, with BJP in the crosshairs. Congress believes these controversies can hurt the governing party politically as they affect a large segment of Indian population and youth.Kharge and Rahul told Modi that he had announced the formation of Shri Ram Janmbhoomi Teerth Kshetra in Parliament, and its members appointed by Centre are closely associated with RSS, VHP and affiliates, while its "disgraced former general secretary" is "your close associate".They said in the letter,"Your silence now in the face of such a crime is unacceptable."The Congress duo said the findings of the independent probe and the trust's accounts should be made public so that every devotee knows how their offerings have been used.There is a consensus among opposition parties that govt should discuss the issue in Parliament, with SP's Dharmendra Yadav, RSP's NK Premachandran, CPI's Sandosh Kumar stressing the demand in the all-party meeting with govt.Ahead of the session, govt's decision to break up Wangchuk's hunger strike through police action has led to the opposition condemning the Modi govt. -
Article Summary: Mallikarjun Kharge and Rahul Gandhi demand independent probe into Ram temple donation issue. Cockroach Janta Party and activist Sonam Wangchuk planned to march to Parliament Monday over paper leaks. Many opposition parties have supported the protest and are likely to turn up at Jantar Mantar for the Parliament gherao.

Article Title: 'We intend to work with PM Modi': Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar announces entry into NDA with 20 Mamata rebels
Article Text:
Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar (ANI file photo)

NEW DELHI: Once a Mamata loyalist and now an NCPI leader after rebelling against the Mamata Banerjee-led Trinamool Congress , Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar on Su nda y announced that she would join the ruling NDA in the Lok Sabha ahead of Monsoon Session. She said 20 former TMC MPs, who have formed a separate parliamentary group under the NCPI, would also join the PM Modi-led alliance in the Lower House.She said the group intends to work with the NDA under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union home minister Amit Shah.The announcement marks a significant step in the Mamata rebels' realignment amid the continuing exodus from the TMC following its crushing defeat to the BJP in the West Bengal assembly elections in May.Addressing the media, Kakoli said the group had decided to back the NDA and work under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union home minister Amit Shah."We, 20 AITC MPs, are joining the NDA alliance. Leaving AITC, we align ourselves with NCPI... we intend to work with the NDA, supporting the initiatives of PM Modi and our Home Minister Amit Shah and be involved in their work of nation-building. We stated in the all-party meeting that we would work together.There are 8 women among our group of 20, and we will stand by the government on whatever issues they take up," she said.Speaking about the NCPI parliamentary party meeting, Kakoli said the group's focus would be on constructive participation during the Monsoon Session."There will be discussions and debates on 5-6 bills... Everyone should debate, but we shouldn't waste the House's time, as that causes significant loss to the country and the public. In the interest of the nation and for the progress of the people, we will debate the upcoming issues, whether they concern taxes or the registration of births and deaths, but we will ensure the House functions smoothly. That is what we discussed," she added.The development comes amid an unprecedented rebellion within the Trinamool Congress after the party, led by Mamata Banerjee , suffered a stinging defeat to the BJP in the West Bengal assembly polls after 15 years in power.While Ritabrata Banerjee has emerged as the face of the rebellion within the party organisation by staking claim to the leader of the opposition post in the assembly against the wishes of the party leadership. Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar also has led a large section of TMC MPs in the Lok Sabha away from the Mamata Banerjee-led camp. The rebel faction has since aligned itself with the NCPI and is now extending support to the BJP-led NDA in Parliament.
Article Summary: Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar has led a large section of TMC MPs away from the Mamata Banerjee-led camp. The rebel faction has since aligned itself with the NCPI and is now extending support to the BJP-led NDA in Parliament.

Article Title: 'NCPI recognised by EC': Sudip Bandyopadhyay hits back after opposition's walkout over TMC rebels' presence
Article Text:
'NCPI is recognised by EC': Sudip Bandyopadhyay hits back after opposition's walkout over TMC rebels' presence

NEW DELHI: Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI) leader Sudip Bandyopadhyay on Sunday defended his party's participation in the all-party meeting ahead of the Monsoon Session, saying the NCPI is recognised by the Election Commission and dismissing the oppposition's protest over the presence of rebel Trinamool Congress (TMC) MPs.His remarks came after several opposition parties staged a symbolic walkout from the all-party meeting to protest the invitation extended to Bandyopadhyay and fellow NCPI leader Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar. The opposition later rejoined the meeting, saying the walkout was meant to register its protest.Speaking to reporters, Bandyopadhyay criticised the opposition's decision to walk out."I have never seen a walkout taking place during an all-party leaders' meeting. The NCPI is recognised by the Election Commission. Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar is going to submit all documents to the Lok Sabha Speaker," he said.The former TMC leader also claimed that his erstwhile party had been reduced in strength after 20 of its Lok Sabha members joined the NCPI."I also demanded (at the all-party meeting) that a small party should not be given seats in the first row. Just eight (MPs) are left in the party (TMC) after its 20 out of 28 moved out," Bandyopadhyay told reporters.Responding to the opposition's objections over the NCPI's participation in the meeting, he said the matter would become clear once Parliament convenes."Whatever they are telling, let them say. Whatever the Lok Sabha Speaker has to say, only tomorrow the Parliament will open and you will find whose seat is where," he said.On the TMC's demand seeking the disqualification of the 20 rebel MPs, Bandyopadhyay said the decision rests entirely with Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla."If the Speaker thinks that it is fit for disqualification, he will disqualify. And if he thinks that from tomorrow we should sit as a separate bloc in the first row, we'll find tomorrow. Wait for 24 hours," he said.He added that Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar would submit all the required documents to the Lok Sabha Speaker on Sunday.Dastidar welcomed the government's decision to invite the NCPI to the all-party meeting."We express our gratitude for being invited to the all-party meeting. It is a sign of respect toward our newly formed coalition of 20 MPs," she said.The all-party meeting convened by the government ahead of the Monsoon Session witnessed a symbolic walkout by opposition parties after Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla approved separate seating for the 20 rebel TMC MPs who have formed the NCPI.TMC MP Mahua Moitra questioned the government's decision to invite the rebel MPs, arguing that the Lok Sabha Speaker had not yet approved their merger and that disqualification petitions against them were still pending.Parliamentary affairs minister Kiren Rijiju defended the invitation, saying, "The NCPI requested recognition from the Lok Sabha Speaker; how can you ignore them? It is the government's duty to invite everyone."The NCPI held its parliamentary party meeting at Samvidhan Sadan on Sunday ahead of the Monsoon Session, which begins on Monday. While Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla has approved separate seating for the 20 rebel TMC MPs.
Article Summary: Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI) leader Sudip Bandyopadhyay defended his party's participation in the all-party meeting ahead of the Monsoon Session. He said the NCPI is recognised by the Election Commission and dismissed the oppposition's protest over the presence of rebel Trinamool Congress (TMC) MPs. His remarks came after several opposition parties staged a symbolic walkout to protest the invitation.

Article Title: Call all-party meet to discuss delimitation bill: Kharge to PM Modi
Article Text:
Poll Vote & Share your view Do you think the reintroduction of the delimitation bill will favor the BJP? Yes No 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: Amid reports that govt is looking to reintroduce delimitation bill in the Monsoon session, Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge Thursday wrote to PM Narendra Modi , urging him to call an all-party meeting to discuss the revised Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill.Kharge wrote that he had repeatedly asked the parliamentary affairs minister for an all-party meeting during the Budget session, but his demand was not heeded, after which the delimitation bill fell in Lok Sabha on April 17 — a reference to the defeat of the govt bill which the opposition termed as being in name for women’s reservation but having the real objective of delimiting Lok Sabha seats on the basis of population to advantage BJP and hurt the southern states.Kharge wrote to Modi that he has been reading media reports about govt move to reintroduce the bill with some changes, urging the PM that he should share the details with the opposition and give it adequate time to study the proposal before it is introduced in Parliament.Kharge’s initiative to settle the heightened speculation over the bill came even as Congress said that despite splitting TMC and Shiv Sena, Modi govt is far from achieving the 2/3rd majority required to pass a constitutional amendment bill.AICC spokesman Jairam Ramesh said Kharge, Rahul Gandhi, and the Congress leadership are in touch with all the parties “that voted against the said bill in April”, and discounted the possibility of DMK and NCP voting in its favour on reintroduction.“The home minister is trying his best to break the parties to achieve the majority mark, but he is far from it. He is attempting to achieve a tainted majority by splitting parties, which is against the people’s mandate and the constitution,” he said.About DMK with which Congress had a bitter falling out, Ramesh said Congress is in touch with the Tamil Nadu party. “They are fundamentally opposed to the ideology of BJP and the way delimitation has been done in Assam and J&K,” Ramesh said, including SP in his remarks.
Article Summary: Mallikarjun Kharge writes to PM Narendra Modi urging him to call an all-party meeting to discuss the revised Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill. Kharge wrote to Modi that he has been reading media reports about govt move to reintroduce the bill with some changes, urging the PM that he should share the details with the opposition.

Article Title: ‘Complaint due to break-up’: 11 years on, man acquitted of harassment charges
Article Text:
Mumbai:

After finding that the woman filed a police complaint against the accused only because he ended their five-year relationship, a magistrate’s court this week acquitted a 35-year-old south Mumbai man who faced 11 years of litigation after being accused of stalking, sending obscene messages, and threatening to unlawfully transmit private photographs of his former girlfriend. While the prosecution claimed the man obtained the complainant’s photographs without her knowledge, the woman stated in evidence that the photographs were clicked by her and sent to the man during the relationship.“This fact clearly goes to show that, the accused did not obtain objectionable photos of the informant without her knowledge, but crystalises that, the informant had sent it, when she was in love-ship with the accused,” the magistrate noted. The magistrate further noted that the complainant admitted that the two dated for about five years and that the man never misbehaved with her during that period. “…the relationship between the informant and the accused which broke after five years of dating has led to this prosecution,” the magistrate said. The prosecution alleged that the man and the complainant had known each other since childhood, studied together and were in a relationship.According to the prosecution, after the complainant became engaged to another person in Oct 2013, the man allegedly threatened to expose private photographs, contacted the fiancé, and later sent abusive and threatening messages along with photographs.The court noted that the chargesheet was filed before receipt of the forensic laboratory report. “It is also clear that, the mobile phone from which messages were sent does not belong to the accused, but it belongs to one Akshay Rathod.”
Article Summary: A 35-year-old south Mumbai man faced 11 years of litigation after being accused of stalking, sending obscene messages, and threatening to unlawfully transmit private photographs of his former girlfriend. While the prosecution claimed the man obtained the complainant’s photographs without her knowledge, the woman stated in evidence that the photographs were clicked by her and sent to the man during the relationship.

Article Title: 14-year-old dies in Airoli hospital, family go to police
Article Text:
Attachment

Navi Mumbai: A 14-year-old schoolboy residing in a chawl in Digha, who was admitted to a private hospital in Airoli node on July 17 after he complained of severe abdominal pain, died after 12 hours of treatment.The parents of the deceased boy, supported by Vanchit Bahujan Aghadi political party workers, lodged a complaint at Rabale MIDC police station against the doctors concerned of the private hospital.When questioned by the VBA workers, the doctors claimed the negligence allegation by the patient’s parents was false.The deceased boy was identified as Prabodhan Shinde. He was a student of Class 9, residing with his parents at Dr Babasaheb Ambedkar chawl, Vishnu Nagar, Digha.
Article Summary: The 14-year-old was admitted to a private hospital in Airoli node on July 17 after he complained of severe abdominal pain. The parents of the deceased boy, supported by Vanchit Bahujan Aghadi political party workers, lodged a complaint at Rabale MIDC police station against the doctors concerned of the private hospital.

Article Title: HC stays Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar town vending committee polls
Article Text:
Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:

Bombay HC’s Aurangabad bench has stayed the election programme for the town vending committee (TVC) in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, observing that there appeared to be prima facie merit in the contention that the labour commissioner could not delegate the power to conduct the poll to the municipal commissioner under the Maharashtra Street Vendors (Protection of Livelihood and Regulation of Street Vending) Rules, 2016.A division bench of Justices Nitin B Suryawanshi and Abasaheb D Shinde passed the interim order on Friday while hearing a writ petition filed by the Shahid Bhagatsingh Hawkers’ Union through its president, advocate Abhay Manohar Taksal, who appeared as party-in-person. The court issued notices to the respondents and scheduled the next hearing for Aug 13.The petition challenges the election programme published on June 29 for the constitution of the TVC. According to the petitioner, Rule 14 of the 2016 Rules permits the labour commissioner to delegate poll-related powers only to a subordinate officer not below the rank of labour officer and not to the commissioner of a municipal corporation.Taking note of the submission, the bench observed that the contention appeared to have merit and ordered, “Until further orders, there shall be stay to the election programme published on 29/06/2026.”The election process had been initiated by Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar Municipal Corporation (CSMC), which had announced polling on July 25. The civic body had proposed voting at six municipal schools via 20 polling booths to elect eight members of the town vending committee.
Article Summary: Bombay HC’s Aurangabad bench has stayed the election programme for the town vending committee (TVC) in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar. A division bench of Justices Nitin B Suryawanshi and Abasaheb D Shinde passed the interim order on Friday while hearing a writ petition filed by the Shahid Bhagatsingh Hawkers’ Union.

Article Title: 7 FIRs filed for theft of 1,750kg copper coils from MSEDCL transformers in Panvel
Article Text:
The thefts were allegedly committed between April and July 6. (AI-generated image)

NAVI MUMBAI: Panvel City Police have registered seven separate FIRs against unidentified persons for allegedly stealing 1,750 kg of copper coils worth Rs 8.75 lakh from MSEDCL transformers kept at different plots in Karanjade node of Panvel taluka. The thefts were allegedly committed between April and July 6. The incident came to light after MSEDCL officials inspected the plots.According to the complainant, Amit Palve (42), assistant engineer with MSEDCL's Pargaon branch in Panvel taluka, the thieves broke open the windows of rooms at seven different plots where the defunct transformers were kept and stole the copper coils from them.When contacted, Palve said, "The seven transformers were defunct and hence kept in the rooms at the respective plots. On July 6, the theft came to light and we informed the Panvel City Police, who carried out a preliminary investigation. As there was no CCTV surveillance in the area, there was no technical evidence. Since it was a major theft, an FIR was registered against unidentified accused under relevant BNS sections for the offences of trespassing and theft. "
Article Summary: Panvel City Police have registered seven separate FIRs against unidentified persons for allegedly stealing 1,750 kg of copper coils worth Rs 8.75 lakh. The thefts were allegedly committed between April and July 6. The incident came to light after MSEDCL officials inspected the plots.

Article Title: DMK MLA GV Markandeyan arrested over alleged threat against Tamil Nadu CM C Joseph Vijay
Article Text:
Police escort Vilathikulam DMK MLA GV Markandeyan after his arrest over alleged remarks against Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay.

CHENNAI: Police on Monday arrested Vilathikulam DMK MLA GV Markandeyan over alleged threatening and controversial remarks against Tamil Nadu chief minister C Joseph Vijay made during a public meeting in Kovilpatti.Videos of the speech have been circulating on social media, drawing criticism over the MLA's remarks.Police reached Markandeyan's residence in Vilathikulam early in the morning and took him into custody.Supporters of the MLA protested the arrest, raised slogans and attempted to block the police vehicle. Police removed the protesters before taking the MLA away.According to officials, Markandeyan was taken to the office of the superintendent of police for questioning and is expected to be produced before a court later.The arrest has triggered political reactions and created a stir in the area.
Article Summary: Vilathikulam DMK MLA GV Markandeyan arrested over alleged threatening and controversial remarks against Tamil Nadu chief minister C Joseph Vijay. Videos of the speech have been circulating on social media, drawing criticism over the MLA's remarks. The arrest has triggered political reactions and created a stir in the area.

Article Title: College student dies in road accident near Krishnagiri
Article Text:
The accident happenednear the Sendrayampatti bus stop on Friday morning





KRISHNAGIRI: A college student died after a Tamil Nadu State Transport Corporation bus rammed his two-wheeler in Krishnagiri on Friday.The deceased has been identified as Madesh, 18, of N Thattakkal village near Pochampalli in Krishnagiri. He was a first-year degree student at a private college.The accident happened when Madesh was returning home from Velampatti after seeing off some relatives. As he was negotiating a narrow, sharp curve near the Sendrayampatti bus stop, a TNSTC bus coming from the opposite direction rammed his motorcycle. Madesh fell under a front wheel of the bus and was crushed to death on the spot.Upon receiving information about the accident, the Nagarasampatti police rushed to the spot, recovered Madesh’s body and sent it to the Krishnagiri Govt Medical College Hospital for postmortem. Police registered a case and began investigations.According to locals, dense tamarind trees line both sides of the road, completely obstructing the view around the narrow curve. Drivers are unable to see oncoming vehicles until it is too late, which they allege was the reason for this accident.
Article Summary: The deceased has been identified as Madesh, 18, of N Thattakkal village near Pochampalli in Krishnagiri. He was a first-year degree student at a private college. The accident happened when Madesh was returning home from Velampatti after seeing off some relatives.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Text:
Police arrested a 29-year-old man and seized 1.5kg of ganja

Madurai: Police arrested a 29-year-old man and seized 1.5kg of ganja allegedly kept for sale within Villur police station limits on Saturday. Acting on information about ganja sales, a patrol team detained Arun Kumar and recovered the contraband from him, Madurai district police said in a statement. Villur police registered a case and are questioning the accused to determine the source of the ganja and whether others were involved in its distribution. TNN
Article Summary: Police arrested a 29-year-old man and seized 1.5kg of ganja allegedly kept for sale. Acting on information, a patrol team detained Arun Kumar and recovered the contraband. Villur police registered a case and are questioning the accused.

Article Title: Elderly woman suffers burn injuries during power outage in Kochi, dies
Article Text:
The fire from a candle the woman lit during the power outage reportedly caught on her dress

Kochi

:

A 71-year-old woman from Kochi, who suffered burn injuries from a candle during a power outage last week, has passed away while undergoing treatment at Kottayam govt medical college hospital (MCH)The deceased has been identified Mariyam Paily, a native of Elamakkara North.Ward councillor Beena Mahesh said Mariyam suffered burns around 8.30pm when there was power outage in the area. “Mariyam who was living with her elder sister, was wearing a cotton night dress. When the power cut took place, she tried to light a candle. The cotton dress that she was wearing reportedly caught on fire. Her sister alerted neighbours who then took her to Ernakulam general hospital. As she suffered severe burn injuries, the hospital referred her to Kottayam MCH,” said Beena.Elamakkara police registered a case of unnatural death under Section 194 of BNS.
Article Summary: Mariyam Paily, 71, died while undergoing treatment at Kottayam govt medical college hospital (MCH) The fire from a candle the woman lit during the power outage reportedly caught on her dress. Elamakkara police registered a case of unnatural death under Section 194 of BNS.
